name: Cangas del Narcea al Santuario del Acebo – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cangas Del Narcea offers diverse and rugged natural landscapes, characterized by extensive river networks and numerous valleys. The region features significant protected areas like the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña, and Ibias Natural Park, which includes dense oak groves and varied terrain. Mountain bikers can expect demanding climbs and scenic descents through forests and along rivers, with elevations reaching over 2,000 meters. This varied landscape provides an exciting destination for mountain biking enthusiasts. Best mountain bike trails around Cangas Del Narcea The most popular mountain bike trail is Camino Primitivo: Salas - Tineo Loop, a 29.3 miles (47.2 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route traverses varied terrain, including sections of the historic Camino Primitivo. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Corias - Santuario del Acebo MTB Trail, a moderate 14.0 miles (22.5 km) path. This trail offers scenic views as it climbs towards the Santuario del Acebo. Local mountain bikers also love the Mirador de Letizia y Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Obana — circular desde Tineo, a 14.9 miles (24.0 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past cultural landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Cangas Del Narcea is defined by dense oak forests, rugged river valleys, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 3 moderate and 17 difficult routes available. The routes in Cangas Del Narcea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cangas Del Narcea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Cangas del Narcea, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Cangas del Narcea offers a robust network of over 20 mountain bike trails. The majority, 17 routes, are classified as difficult, providing challenging experiences for seasoned riders. Additionally, there are 3 moderate routes available, suitable for intermediate mountain bikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Mountain biking in Cangas del Narcea features diverse and rugged natural landscapes. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with demanding climbs and scenic descents through dense oak forests, along rivers, and across varied terrain. The region is characterized by extensive river networks and numerous valleys, offering a dynamic riding experience.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Yes, Cangas del Narcea is known for its challenging routes. For experienced riders, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482349/pola-de-allande-al-puerto-de-palo-y-la-marta-circular-en-el-camino-primitivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Allande al Puerto de Palo y La Marta – circular en el Camino Primitivo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 47.9 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027481557/cangas-del-narcea-al-santuario-del-acebo-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas del Narcea al Santuario del Acebo – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 38.9 km difficult route that climbs towards the Santuario del Acebo.

Q: What natural features or protected areas can I explore while mountain biking in Cangas del Narcea?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, including the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña, and Ibias Natural Park, a Biosphere Reserve with extensive oak groves. You can also find the Muniellos Integral Natural Reserve, home to Spain's largest oak grove, and the Cueto de Arbas Partial Nature Reserve, featuring a glacial lake at the foot of Cueto de Arbas mountain. These areas offer stunning backdrops for your rides.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer rewarding views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206153076/mirador-de-letizia-y-monasterio-de-santa-maria-la-real-de-obana-circular-desde-tineo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Letizia y Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Obana — circular desde Tineo&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the Mirador de Letizia, offering panoramic vistas. Other notable viewpoints in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Navia River and the Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What cultural or historical attractions can I visit near the mountain bike routes?
A: Cangas del Narcea boasts a rich cultural heritage. The Monastery of Corias, often referred to as the 'Escorial of Asturias,' is a significant historical site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206153076/mirador-de-letizia-y-monasterio-de-santa-maria-la-real-de-obana-circular-desde-tineo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Letizia y Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Obana — circular desde Tineo&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Obana. You can also explore charming villages like Bisuyu, known for its literary connections.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Cangas del Narcea?
A: The mountain bike routes in Cangas del Narcea are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 4 reviews. Riders frequently praise the region's varied terrain, the challenging climbs, and the scenic descents through forests and along rivers, highlighting the exciting and diverse experience it offers.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that are circular routes?
A: Yes, several routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482349/pola-de-allande-al-puerto-de-palo-y-la-marta-circular-en-el-camino-primitivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Allande al Puerto de Palo y La Marta – circular en el Camino Primitivo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027481557/cangas-del-narcea-al-santuario-del-acebo-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas del Narcea al Santuario del Acebo – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; are both designed as loops, bringing you back to your starting point.

Q: Can I observe wildlife, such as bears, while mountain biking in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Cangas del Narcea is a significant area for wildlife, particularly the Cantabrian brown bear. The Muniellos Integral Natural Reserve, within the Fuentes del Narcea park, is a crucial refuge for these animals. While direct encounters on trails are rare, the presence of these magnificent creatures adds to the wild character of the region, and designated bear lookout points exist in the wider area.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs accessible near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region features several beautiful water bodies. The Cueto de Arbas Partial Nature Reserve has a spectacular glacial lake. Additionally, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7661408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Barca&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7735890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilotuerto Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity, offering scenic spots that might be incorporated into longer rides or visited separately.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cangas del Narcea?
A: The diverse landscapes of Cangas del Narcea offer good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be excellent, especially in higher elevations, though some lower-lying areas might be warmer. Winter riding is possible but may be limited by snow and ice, particularly on higher mountain passes.

Q: Are there any mountain passes that are popular for mountain biking in the area?
A: Yes, the region is known for its mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5371946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Leitariegos&lt;/a&gt; is a classic ascent, historically featured in cycling events like the Vuelta a Asturias. Other passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Connio Pass&lt;/a&gt; also offer challenging climbs and rewarding views.
tips: Cangas del Narcea is the largest and oldest municipality in Asturias. It has numerous wineries, a Wine Museum and a very interesting historic quarter. The Monasterio de Corias is worth seeing - it is now converted into a ''National Parador''.

A beautiful parish known especially for the Monastery of San Juan Bautista which today houses a ''National Tourism Parador''. It has a unique microclimate in Asturias, because it is not near the sea and because of the peculiar orography of the area. This makes Corias summers very hot, but on the opposite side, winters are very cold.

At this point we find the end of the climb to the Holly Sanctuary, a climb that takes us to an altitude of 1,169 masl with a drop of 768m and a length of 9.39 km. The average % is 8.18 and the maximum % is 16. A true port for lovers of road cycling. The sanctuary is a popular destination for pilgrims and tourists who want to admire its beautiful architecture and history.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Acebo
